content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901              322-6441                 322-6444

March 27, 1984


Lowell Smith
Mayor, City of St. Helena
1480 Main Street
St. Helena, CA 94574

Re: Advice Letter No. 84-063

Dear Mayor Smith:

     This letter is in response to the questions you raised
during our telephone conversation on March 15, 1984.

FACTS

     Recently, the Christian Brothers Winery was closed because
it is structurally unsound. A developer is considering buying
the winery and converting it into a hotel. You have been
offered an investment interest in this development.

QUESTIONS PRESENTED

1.    Does the Political Reform Act allow you to speak to the
press and to civic groups about the proposal?

CONCLUSION

     If you acquire an investment interest in the development,
you will not violate the Political Reform Act by speaking to the
press or civic groups.

DISCUSSION

     As you are aware, Government Code Section 87100 1/
prohibits a local public official from making, participating in
the making, or in any way attempting to use his official

_____________________

1/ Hereinafter all statutory references are to the
Government Code, unless otherwise indicated.,

Lowell Smith
March 27, 1984
Page 2


position to influence2/ a governmental decision in which he
knows or has reason to know that he has a financial interest.
An official has a "financial interest" in a decision if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect3/ on:

     (a)  Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct ... investment worth more than
one thousand dollars ($1,000).

     (b)  Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth more
than one thousand dollars ($1,000).

                         (Section 87103 (a) and (b).)

     If you acquire an investment interest of $1,000 or more in
the business entity developing the hotel, you may not make,
participate in, or use your official position to influence a
any decision which will foreseeably have a material financial
effect on the interest.  (See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18700
and 18702.)4/ An official "uses his official position to
influence a decision" if he furthers or attempts to affect in
any manner any decision:

          (1)  Within or before his ... agency; or

_______________________

     2/ For the definitions of these phrases, see the enclosed
copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700.

     3/ For an explanation of the phrase "material financial
effect," see the enclosed copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18702.

     4/ If you acquire a 10% or greater interest in the
business entity developing the hotel, you will also have a
pro rata ownership interest in any real property held by the
entity. You will be required to disqualify yourself from
decisions which will materially affect any of these real
property interests. You will, of course, also be required to
disqualify yourself from other decisions having a direct
material financial effect on the development company.

Lowell Smith
March 27, 1984
Page 3


     (2) Before any agency which is appointed by or
subject to the budgetary control of his ... agency.

(2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18700(e).)

     If you speak with the press or civic groups about the
proposed hotel development, you will not be using your official
position to influence the governmental decisions concerning the
proposed development. This phase is not intended to prevent
such discussion with the public. I have included copies of the
Harron (A-83-184) and Thacher (A-83-226) advice letters which
discuss the types of activities which are prohibited.

     If I can be of any help in the future, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Janis Shank McLean
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

JSM:plh
Enclosures

press and to civic groups about the proposal?
